findings.problem: The chapter's central catastrophe keeps action ownership hedged at the decisive beats, so culpability reads as atmospheric rather than fully causal. The text repeatedly implies Kwon may or may not be responsible, but still drives the event forward without one unambiguous command chain the reader can pin as the trigger.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:61, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:83, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:325, add one explicit operational sequence (credential holder -> refused purge -> keep-on-camera order) so motive can remain contested while causal responsibility is explicit.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can point to two explicit lines in the revised cited spans that show who authorized continued contaminated display and who ordered continued on-camera exposure; FAIL if responsibility still depends mainly on inference from ambiguity language.

findings.problem: Sanitization drift in the aftermath: once the riot flips, the narrative leans on hashtag metrics, clip counts, and predictive summary language at the exact point the premise calls for sustained embodied consequence. The chapter gives flashes of harm, then retreats into mediated abstraction.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the aftermath run between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:537 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:618, replace part of the trend-summary forecast with one continuous on-the-ground consequence beat (injury triage, police containment, or custody escalation) observed directly by Ina before returning to strategic planning.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span contains at least one contiguous scene-level consequence sequence (person + injury/constraint + immediate institutional response) shown in real time; FAIL if the section remains primarily dashboard/reporting language about what is trending or what will happen later.

findings.problem: Timidity at the emotional hinge: when Ji-eun asks what Ina put her inside, the confession stays abstract ("a messaging operation") instead of naming concrete choices Ina made that implicated Ji-eun. This softens the betrayal impact right at the pressure peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:351, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:357, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:359, replace abstract framing with concrete culpability language (specific script decisions, approvals, or withheld warnings) and a brief embodied reaction from Ina.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Ina's revised response in this exchange names at least two concrete actions she personally took and Ji-eun responds to those actions; FAIL if the confession still relies on broad labels without specific acts.

findings.problem: Institutional antagonists converge into the same polished bureaucratic cadence under pressure, which blurs idiolect separation. Yoon, Bae, and the yard guard all land in similarly formal control-language, reducing dialogue realism and character distinctness in a panic sequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate registers in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:379, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:383, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:449, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:477 by giving each speaker distinct rhythm and lexical priorities (media-handler spin for Yoon, contractual precision for Bae, terse field-security slang for the guard), with contractions for non-ceremonial lines.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised cited lines, three unattributed lines (one each from Yoon, Bae, and guard) remain clearly attributable by syntax/word choice alone and at least two non-legal panic lines use contractions; FAIL if voices still read as interchangeable formal policy speech.

findings.problem: Stress is repeatedly signaled with the same vocal-collapse construction (voice shook/cracking/cracked/shaking). The recurrence becomes a mechanical default and flattens emotional texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:449, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:599 by replacing at least two voice-failure tags with non-redundant stress markers (breath pattern, gesture failure, lexical fracture, or sensory spill) specific to each speaker.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the four cited lines no longer use voice-shake/crack language and still communicate escalation clearly; FAIL if all four retain the same vocal-distress cue family.

findings.problem: A recurring metaphor template (abstract or procedural noun rendered as a blunt physical object via 'like a/an ...') appears repeatedly and starts to read as formula rather than choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:73, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:155, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:401, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:651, keep only the strongest metaphor instance and rewrite the others with scene-specific concrete action or sensory detail that does not rely on the same simile chassis.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one of the cited lines keeps the current abstract-noun + 'like a/an' impact metaphor pattern; FAIL if three or more of those lines still use the same template.

findings.problem: There is a 10+ paragraph screenplay-like stretch with no focalizer interiority, violating close-third pressure. The sedation corridor exchange runs on external dialogue/action only until later, weakening Ina-centered narrative distance.
findings.rewrite_direction: Within the corridor block anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:379 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:401, insert concise interior beats (thought/sensation/self-assessment) at entry, midpoint, and pre-breakpoint to keep close-third continuity without slowing pace.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if this revised corridor sequence contains at least three explicit interior markers from Ina and no run longer than six consecutive paragraphs without interior experience; FAIL if the block still reads as external-only dialogue/action for 10+ paragraphs.

summary: Chapter 9 lands strong momentum and immediate stakes, but fails review due unresolved causal-accountability blur at the hijack command layer, sanitization/timidity drift in key aftermath/confession beats, dialogue register convergence under pressure, and multiple prose-level repetition/exteriority tics. No canonical continuity contradiction or sustained narrative-tense drift was detected.
